grammarInScope: Pragmatic nuances: por que 'quisiera' suena mas cortes que 'queria'
Sociolinguistic variation: vos vs tu vs usted across Spanish-speaking countries
Pragmatic values of verb tenses (presente historico, futuro de probabilidad, imperfecto de cortesia)
Register differentiation: espanol escrito formal vs oral formal vs coloquial vs literario

inappropriateExerciseTypes.reason: Mechanical fill-in-the-blank too simple unless content is genuinely C1-level (e.g., subtle subjunctive nuances)
Mechanical multiple choice too simple for C1 cognitive level
Pure conjugation paradigm drilling no longer the pedagogical focus; C1 students have internalized the system
Word lists without context insufficient for C1 lexical depth; collocations and register are the goal
Crossword too cognitively simple for C1 level
Word search too cognitively simple for C1 level
Memory/pairs too childish for C1 cognitive level

vocabularyApproach: Depth over quantity: collocations of the same root, register variants, connotations. Student already has broad vocabulary; objective is precision and richness, not item count.
metalanguageLevel: Metalanguage is a learning tool, not an obstacle. Full academic, rhetorical, and stylistic terminology.
errorCorrection: Mostly deferred. Student self-corrects with teacher cues. Recasting. Focus on register and style precision rather than grammatical accuracy alone.

guidedWriting.structures: wide range including nominalization, relative clauses, passive voice
guidedWriting.complexity: Complex abstract topics. Cohesive multi-paragraph text.
